---
title: "fitmodel_assignment2"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
pacman::p_load(here, cmdstanr, tidyverse, posterior)

library(cmdstanr)
```

```{r}
data_raw <- read_csv("stan_test_data.csv")

data_for_stan <- list(n = nrow(data_raw), choice = data_raw$matcher_choices, reward = data_raw$outcomes_matcher)
```


```{r}
## --- Compile and Fit ---
# Specify model file path
stan_file_RL <- here("2ndassignment_clean.stan")

# Define path for saving fitted model object
stan_results_dir <- "fitted_models"
model_file_RL <- here("2ndassigment_fitted.rds")

if (!dir.exists(stan_results_dir)) dir.create(stan_results_dir)

regenerate_simulations = TRUE

if (regenerate_simulations || !file.exists(model_file_RL)) {
  cat("Compiling and fitting Stan model (RL)...\n")
  # Compile the Stan model (only needs to be done once unless code changes)
  mod_RL <- cmdstan_model(stan_file_RL,
                                 cpp_options = list(stan_threads = FALSE), # Enable threading
                                 stanc_options = list("O1")) # Basic optimization

  # Sample from the posterior distribution using MCMC
  fit_RL <- mod_RL$sample(
    data = data_for_stan,          # The data list we prepared
    seed = 123,                    # For reproducible MCMC sampling
    chains = 4,                    # Number of parallel Markov chains (recommend 4)
    parallel_chains = 4, # Run chains in parallel
    #threads_per_chain = 1,         # For within-chain parallelization (usually 1 is fine)
    iter_warmup = 1000,            # Number of warmup iterations per chain (discarded)
    iter_sampling = 2000,          # Number of sampling iterations per chain (kept)
    refresh = 0,                   # How often to print progress updates. 0 means never since the model is so fast
    max_treedepth = 10,            # Controls complexity of MCMC steps (adjust if needed)
    adapt_delta = 0.8              # Target acceptance rate (adjust if divergences occur)
  )

  # Save the fitted model object
  fit_RL$save_object(file = model_file_RL)
  cat("Model fit completed and saved to:", model_file_RL, "\n")
} else {
  # Load existing fitted model object
  fit_RL <- readRDS(model_file_RL)
  cat("Loaded existing model fit from:", model_file_RL, "\n")
}
```
```{r}
# 2. Check R-hat and ESS metrics
fit_summary <- fit_RL$summary(variables = "alpha")
print(fit_summary %>% select(variable, mean, rhat, ess_bulk, ess_tail))
```

```{r}
model_draws <- as_draws_df(fit_RL)
```

```{r}
hist(model_draws)
```
